title: hier_block mode module has wrong port connection order
body:
When I compile the below case with `verilator -cc dut.v --hierarchical --debug` (verilator version: 4.225)
### dut.v
```
`timescale 1ps/1ps

module mod2 (
    output reg q,
    input wire d,
    input wire iclk
);
    
    /*verilator hier_block*/
    
    always@(posedge iclk)
        q = d;
    
endmodule

module dut (
    output wire q, 
    input wire d,
    input wire clk
);

    mod2 u_mod (q, d, clk);

endmodule

```
Will get error message:
```
%Error-ASSIGNIN: dut.v:21:20: Assigning to input/const variable: 'd'
-node: VARREF 0xf01420 <e519#> {e21au} @dt=0@  d [LV] => VAR 0xf17620 <e355> {e17aq} @dt=0@  d INPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
   21 |     mod2 u_mod (q, d, clk);
      |                    ^
                 ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/ASSIGNIN?v=4.225
```
Here's what I found in a debug message file:
### Vdut_007_linkjump.tree
```
(omit)
1:2: CELL 0x2a4a620 <e376> {e21ak}  u_mod -> MODULE 0x2a3a540 <e400#> {d6ai}  mod2  L3 [1ps]
1:2:1: PIN 0x2a49ff0 <e365> {e21ar}  __pinNumber1 -> VAR 0x2a273e0 <e13> {d7av} @dt=0@  iclk INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x2a33310 <e516#> {e21ar} @dt=0@  q [RV] <- VAR 0x2a49100 <e349> {e16ar} @dt=0@  q OUTPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
1:2:1: PIN 0x2a4a290 <e370> {e21au}  __pinNumber2 -> VAR 0x2a27950 <e21> {d8ay} @dt=0@  q OUTPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x2a33420 <e519#> {e21au} @dt=0@  d [LV] => VAR 0x2a49620 <e355> {e17aq} @dt=0@  d INPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
1:2:1: PIN 0x2a4a530 <e374> {e21ax}  __pinNumber3 -> VAR 0x2a27e70 <e30> {d9ax} @dt=0@  d INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
1:2:1:1: VARREF 0x2a33530 <e522#> {e21ax} @dt=0@  clk [RV] <- VAR 0x2a49b40 <e362> {e18aq} @dt=0@  clk INPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
(omit)
```
You can see that the pin order is different from the order in the **dut.v**. In the **Vdut_007_linkjump.tree**, the `mod2`'s pin order is `iclk -> q -> d`, but in the **dut.v**, the `mod2`'s pin order is `q -> d -> iclk`. I think it's the cause of the **ASSIGNIN** error.

I also found that if I change to put the `d` in the always block like `always@(posedge d)` instead of `always@(posedge iclk)`, the `__pinNumber1`  will be `d` instead of `iclk`.

Is this a bug or is that I have to add some options to prevent this error? Thanks
